stem: The general second-degree equation \( [[A]]x^{2}+[[B]]xy+[[C]]y^{2}+[[D]]x+[[E]]y+[[F]]=0 \) is classified by the sign of its discriminant \( B^{2}-4AC \) (negative gives an ellipse, positive a hyperbola, zero a parabola). Compute \( B^{2}-4AC \) for this equation.
solution: The classification discriminant is \( B^{2}-4AC=([[B]])^{2}-4([[A]])([[C]])=[[answer]] \); its sign (not the linear terms \( D,E,F\)) determines the conic's type.
distractor_rationales: This adds \( 4AC\) instead of subtracting it.
This swaps the roles of \( A,B,C\) in the formula.
This mistakenly folds in the linear coefficients \( D,E\), which do not affect the discriminant.
This swaps \( A\) and \( B\) in the formula.
This is off by one from the correct discriminant.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct discriminant.
